mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months following a rate decline, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.0 percent, weak labor demand suppresses consumer spending and corporate profit growth, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 8.0, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand expansion, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative 20-day returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During post-recession recovery phases, a sustained rise in the index of industrial production signals capacity utilization improvements and margin expansion, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >55% of recovery-phase observations, or the mechanism fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 100 basis points annually.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product reflects underlying economic expansion that translates into higher corporate revenues, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no significant correlation with GDPC1 growth rates (Rank IC < 0.01) across rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 235.0, rising inflation expectations compress real yields and trigger equity risk premium repricing, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 20-day returns in >60% of months where CPIAUCSL crosses the 235.0 threshold, or the threshold effect vanishes in quantile regression testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and declines in the unemployment rate confirm labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces negative 60-day returns in >50% of observations, or the interaction effect fails to achieve a positive information coefficient in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: In monetary tightening cycles, strong retail sales and GDP growth signal economic overheating that prompts central bank rate hikes, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >55% of tightening-cycle observations with strong demand data, or the regime-specific alpha decays to zero within 12 months.
